title: China-to-Bahamas Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: EMC and Harmonics — 60 Hz Bahamas Grid, IEEE / UL Direction, and Project Evidence
rows.target_requirement: For Bahamas PV projects, EMC and power-quality evidence should be prepared for a 60 Hz, 120/240 V grid and for the interconnection documentation expected by BPL, the project engineer, and the authority having jurisdiction. IEEE 1547 and UL 1741 style inverter evidence may be requested because The Bahamas uses a US-heritage NEMA ecosystem. IEC 62920 and IEC 61000-series EMC evidence can still be useful as technical support, but exporters should not assume a 50 Hz China GB/T 37408 EMC package or European EN 50549/VDE grid package is sufficient without 60 Hz project review.
target_standards: IEEE 1547 — distributed energy resource interconnection and interoperability, including power-quality and response functions
UL 1741 — inverter and interconnection-system equipment certification direction that may be requested for US-heritage 60 Hz systems
IEC 62920 — Photovoltaic power generating systems — EMC requirements and test methods for power conversion equipment (supporting evidence where accepted)
IEC 61000 series — EMC and harmonic test methods and limits, subject to project acceptance
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese PV inverter EMC files commonly rely on GB/T 37408-2021, NB/T 32004, and CNAS laboratory reports generated under China domestic voltage and frequency assumptions. These reports may document emissions, immunity, and harmonics for a China 220/380 V, 50 Hz configuration. They do not automatically prove operation or power-quality compliance on a Bahamas 120/240 V, 60 Hz circuit or under IEEE 1547 / UL 1741 project expectations.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters), including EMC-related requirements
NB/T 32004 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
CNAS-accredited China domestic EMC and power-quality test reports
rows.gap: Gap: GB/T 37408 EMC evidence should be screened against the Bahamas installation configuration. Exporters should identify whether the inverter will be supplied as split-phase 120/240 V, three-phase 60 Hz, or through transformer-coupled architecture; confirm harmonic and conducted/radiated EMC evidence under the relevant 60 Hz operating mode; and obtain IEEE 1547 / UL 1741 or IEC 62920 / IEC 61000 supplemental reports where the project reviewer requires them. Re-testing may be needed if existing EMC reports are limited to 50 Hz China grid conditions or a materially different wiring configuration.

mandatory_status.target: Project-dependent but commonly required in practice for BPL/project-owner technical acceptance, especially where IEEE 1547, UL 1741, harmonics, or EMC reports are specified in procurement or interconnection documents
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese EMC reports may support domestic voluntary certification but do not automatically satisfy Bahamas 60 Hz project evidence requirements

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Do not treat a China 50 Hz GB/T 37408 EMC package as complete Bahamas evidence. Map the supplied inverter configuration to the Bahamas 120/240 V, 60 Hz installation and confirm whether the reviewer expects IEEE 1547 / UL 1741, IEC 62920, IEC 61000-series harmonics, or other EMC reports before project submission.

source.publisher: Bahamas Bureau of Standards and Quality (BBSQ)

rows.requirement: Bahamas BPL Grid Interconnection — 120/240 V, 60 Hz NEMA-System Revalidation
rows.target_requirement: Solar PV inverters connected in The Bahamas must be configured for a US-heritage low-voltage grid, commonly 120/240 V at 60 Hz, and for Bahamas Power and Light (BPL) project interconnection review. Because the market is 60 Hz, grid-support and anti-islanding evidence should be framed in the Americas direction, commonly IEEE 1547 for distributed energy resource interconnection and UL 1741 for inverter certification, where required by the project owner, utility, insurer, or authority having jurisdiction. European 50 Hz framing such as EN 50549 or VDE-AR-N 4105 should not be assumed to satisfy Bahamas interconnection expectations.
target_standards: Bahamas Power and Light (BPL) — project-level electrical interconnection review and utility requirements
IEEE 1547 — Standard for Interconnection and Interoperability of Distributed Energy Resources with Associated Electric Power Systems Interfaces (likely Americas-direction reference)
UL 1741 — Inverters, Converters, Controllers and Interconnection System Equipment for Use With Distributed Energy Resources (may be requested for US-heritage systems)
Bahamas utility supply context — commonly 120/240 V, 60 Hz, NEMA-style equipment ecosystem
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese grid-connection evidence commonly includes GB/T 37408-2021, NB/T 32004, and GB/T 19964-2024 evidence developed for China domestic grid conditions. China is a 220/380 V, 50 Hz market. Even where a Chinese inverter has anti-islanding, voltage/frequency protection, ride-through, and reconnection test evidence, those settings are not automatically transferable to a 120/240 V, 60 Hz Bahamas grid or to IEEE 1547 / UL 1741 style project expectations.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
NB/T 32004 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 19964-2024 — 光伏发电站接入电力系统技术规定 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System)
GB/T 156 — 标准电压 (Standard voltages), China 220/380 V 50 Hz context
rows.gap: Gap: this is a fundamental voltage and frequency mismatch. China 220/380 V, 50 Hz validation does not prove Bahamas 120/240 V, 60 Hz interconnection compatibility. Exporters should prepare a Bahamas configuration dossier covering 60 Hz firmware, IEEE 1547 / UL 1741 evidence where requested, anti-islanding trip times, voltage/frequency windows, ride-through settings, reconnection delays, NEMA-compatible wiring and terminals, and BPL project-specific approval. Any model previously tested only under Chinese 50 Hz settings should be revalidated at 60 Hz before shipment or project submission.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory in practice for BPL interconnection and project acceptance; the exact document set should be confirmed with BPL, URCA-regulated utility requirements, the project engineer, and the authority having jurisdiction
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese GB/T grid-connection evidence may support China domestic acceptance but does not transfer to Bahamas 60 Hz BPL interconnection acceptance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat Bahamas PV inverter interconnection as a 60 Hz Americas-style review, not a 50 Hz China or Europe review. Revalidate Chinese GB/T 37408 / NB/T 32004 / GB/T 19964 evidence for 120/240 V, 60 Hz operation, and confirm whether IEEE 1547 and UL 1741 evidence is required by BPL, the project owner, or the authority having jurisdiction before submitting equipment.

source.publisher: Bahamas Power and Light Company Ltd. (BPL)

rows.requirement: Regulatory and Project-Gate Review — URCA Oversight, BBSQ Standards Context, and Hurricane-Prone Island Conditions
rows.target_requirement: The Bahamas electricity sector is overseen by the Utilities Regulation and Competition Authority (URCA), while the Bahamas Bureau of Standards and Quality (BBSQ) is the national standards body. PV inverter acceptance is therefore not just a factory certificate question: projects may need to satisfy utility interconnection review, applicable national standards or adopted standards, local electrical inspection practice, procurement requirements, and insurer or financier requirements. Because The Bahamas is a hurricane-prone island market, outdoor PV equipment packages should also document enclosure ratings, corrosion resistance, surge protection coordination, mounting interface assumptions, and environmental suitability for coastal exposure.
target_standards: Utilities Regulation and Competition Authority (URCA) — electricity-sector regulatory oversight in The Bahamas
Bahamas Bureau of Standards and Quality (BBSQ) — national standards and quality infrastructure
Bahamas Power and Light (BPL) — project-specific grid interconnection and utility acceptance
Project-owner, insurer, and local authority requirements for hurricane-prone coastal installations
rows.china_equivalent: China domestic PV inverter files often focus on factory type tests, CNAS laboratory reports, CQC/CGC voluntary certification, and China grid-code compatibility. These files usually do not address Bahamas utility approval workflow, URCA-regulated electricity-sector context, BBSQ standards status, NEMA-style installation practice, coastal corrosion exposure, hurricane wind-event assumptions, or insurer-driven documentation.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
NB/T 32004 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
CQC/CGC voluntary certification schemes for China PV inverter products
rows.gap: Gap: Chinese documentation should be supplemented with a Bahamas project file that identifies the utility interconnection path, applicable BBSQ or adopted standards, URCA or licence-related constraints if relevant, local inspection requirements, 60 Hz settings, NEMA-compatible installation details, enclosure and surge-protection evidence, and hurricane/coastal environmental suitability. Do not rely on a China domestic certificate package as the sole project approval file.

mandatory_status.target: Project-dependent but mandatory in practice where utility, regulator, inspector, owner, financier, or insurer acceptance is required before energisation
mandatory_status.cn: China domestic documentation is not structured around Bahamas BBSQ / URCA / BPL project acceptance and should be treated only as supporting evidence

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Build a Bahamas-specific project dossier rather than submitting China domestic certificates alone. Confirm the BBSQ standards position, URCA-regulated electricity-sector constraints, BPL interconnection route, and hurricane/coastal environmental documentation expectations with the project engineer and relevant authority before procurement.

rows.requirement: PV Inverter Electrical Safety — IEC 62109 Baseline, UL 1741 Expectations, and NEMA Installation Fit
rows.target_requirement: For Bahamas PV projects, IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 safety evidence remains a useful international baseline for PV inverter electrical safety. However, because The Bahamas uses a US-heritage 120/240 V, 60 Hz, NEMA-style ecosystem, project owners, utilities, insurers, or inspectors may request UL 1741 inverter evidence, NEMA-compatible ratings and installation details, and documentation for field wiring, grounding, disconnects, surge protection, and enclosure suitability. Outdoor equipment should also be reviewed for coastal corrosion, high humidity, salt mist, and hurricane-prone service conditions.
target_standards: IEC 62109-1 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 1: General requirements
IEC 62109-2 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 2: Particular requirements for inverters
UL 1741 — Inverters, Converters, Controllers and Interconnection System Equipment for Use With Distributed Energy Resources (may be requested for US-heritage systems)
NEMA-style installation ecosystem — enclosure, wiring, disconnect, and field-installation compatibility as specified by the project reviewer
rows.china_equivalent: China PV inverter safety and construction files commonly rely on GB/T 37408-2021, NB/T 32004, manufacturer declarations, CNAS test reports, and CQC/CGC voluntary certification. These documents may show China domestic safety compliance for a 220/380 V, 50 Hz product configuration. They are not automatic evidence of IEC 62109-1/-2 certification, UL 1741 certification, NEMA installation suitability, or Bahamas coastal/hurricane environmental fitness.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
NB/T 32004 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
CQC/CGC voluntary certification schemes and CNAS laboratory reports for China domestic PV inverter products
rows.gap: Gap: exporters should supplement Chinese safety files with an international/Bahamas project package. At minimum, confirm IEC 62109-1/-2 model-family coverage, 60 Hz nameplate and firmware configuration, NEMA-compatible wiring and enclosure details, grounding and disconnect instructions, surge-protection coordination, and coastal environmental ratings. Where the reviewer asks for UL 1741, Chinese GB/T evidence cannot substitute. If the same hardware is offered in both China 50 Hz and Bahamas 60 Hz variants, the test report and nameplate file should clearly identify the Bahamas variant.

mandatory_status.target: Project-dependent but commonly mandatory in practice through utility, inspector, owner, insurer, or procurement requirements; UL 1741 is not assumed universally mandatory but should be checked for each Bahamas project
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary or domestic project-driven; China GB/T safety evidence does not automatically satisfy Bahamas IEC 62109, UL 1741, NEMA, or environmental documentation expectations

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Use IEC 62109-1/-2 as the baseline safety file, then confirm whether UL 1741 and NEMA-style installation documentation are required for the specific Bahamas project. Chinese GB/T 37408 or NB/T 32004 safety evidence should be treated as supporting material only unless the project reviewer expressly accepts it.

source.publisher: Bahamas Power and Light Company Ltd. (BPL)
